chunks.text: Kay Ousterhout chosen for Google Ph.D. Fellowship - EECS at Berkeley Kay Ousterhout recently won the Google Ph.d. Fellowship. This is one of the highest honors a CS grad student can win. It is extremely selective, with only a small number universities invited to submit two nominees each. Kay is a 5th year student of Prof. Sylvia Ratnasamy . The Google Phd Fellowship was created in 2009 to recognize and support outstanding graduate students doing exceptional work in Computer Science (CS) and related disciplines. Categories Honors Industry Students View Open Faculty Positions
